fundamentals.name: AST SpaceMobile, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: AST SpaceMobile, Inc., together with its subsidiaries, designs and develops the constellation of BlueBird satellites in the United States. The company provides a cellular broadband network in space to be accessible directly by smartphones for commercial use and other applications, as well as for government use. Its SpaceMobile service provides cellular broadband services to end-users who are out of terrestrial cellular coverage. The company was founded in 2017 and is headquartered in Midland, Texas.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing revenue but still loss-making with a high valuation and significant operational losses.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed EPS estimates by a wide margin (-0.66 vs -0.20); next earnings date is 2026-08-10.
positives: Revenue growth 1952.2% YoY indicates massive top-line expansion.
Strong liquidity with $3.03B cash and current/quick ratios well above 1.
Gross margin of 44.8% suggests good unit economics on services rendered.
Analyst growth estimates for the current quarter (43.5%) and fiscal year (7.9%) are positive.
negatives: Operating margin of -1014.0% and profit margin of 0.0% show significant ongoing losses.
Free cash flow is negative $1.41B TTM, indicating substantial cash burn.
Forward P/E of -356.28 and Price/Sales of 483.74 represent a very high valuation.
Return on equity of -37.8% and return on assets of -6.3% highlight poor profitability metrics.

news.paragraph: AST SpaceMobile saw its stock jump 7% on May 22nd, following broader positive momentum in the space sector driven by companies like Firefly Aerospace and SpaceX. This sector-wide rally, fueled by investor enthusiasm for space stocks, lifted ASTS along with peers like Rocket Lab and Planet Labs. The positive sentiment suggests a favorable environment for space-related equities, with ASTS participating in the upward trend.
